resource_references.text: # 11.物理 > 来源: 教育部《义务教育课程标准（2022年版）》 义务教育 物理课程标准 (2022 年版 ) 作用，必须坚持马克思主义的指导地位，体现马克思主义中国化最新 成果，体现中国和中华民族风格，体现党和国家对教育的基本要求， 体现国家和民族基本价值观，体现人类文化知识积累和创新成果。 义务教育课程规定了教育目标、教育内容和教学基本要求，体现 国家意志，在立德树人中发挥着关键作用。2001 年颁布的《义务教 育课程设置实验方案》和 2011 年颁布的义务教育各课程标准，坚持 了正确的改革方向，体现了先进的教育理念，为基础教育质量提高作 出了积极贡献。随着义务教育全面普及，教育需求从“有学上”转向 “上好学”，必须进一步明确“培养什么人、怎样培养人、为谁培养 人”，优化学校育人蓝图。当今世界科技进步日新月异，网络新媒体 迅速普及，人们生活、学习、工作方式不断改变，儿童青少年成长环 境深刻变化，人才培养面临新挑战。义务教育课程必须与时俱进，进 行修订完善 以习近平新时代中国特色社会主义思想为指导，全面贯彻党的教 育方针，遵循教育教学规律，落实立德树人根本任务，发展素质教 育。

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 流体压强与流速关系

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: middle
- **年级**: 8
- **章节/领域**: 运动和力
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 了解流体压强与流速的关系及其在生产生活中的应用。
> 例 9 了解铁路站台上设置安全线的必要性。

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「流体压强与流速关系」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「流体压强与流速关系」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「流体压强与流速关系」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「流体压强与流速关系」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“了解流体压强与流速的关系及其在生产生活中的应用。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「流体压强与流速关系」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 围绕课标要点“了解流体压强与流速的关系及其在生产生活中的应用。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「流体压强与流速关系」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“了解流体压强与流速的关系及其在生产生活中的应用。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系工程、能源、交通或日常生活中的相关现象，讨论「流体压强与流速关系」的应用边界。
- 补充科学史或技术应用案例，帮助学生理解物理规律如何服务真实问题。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
流体压强与流速关系是物理学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“了解流体压强与流速的关系及其在生产生活中的应用。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以可观察实验或生活现象引入「流体压强与流速关系」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 探究建构：强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“了解流体压强与流速的关系及其在生产生活中的应用。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

deep_textbook_snippets.text: a. Energy can neither be created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction. b. If two systems, A and B, are in thermal equilibrium with each another, and B is in thermal equilibrium with a third system, C, then A is also in thermal equilibrium with C. c. Entropy of any isolated system not in thermal equilibrium always increases. d. Entropy of a system approaches a constant value as temperature approaches absolute zero. 12.2 First law of Thermodynamics: Thermal Energy and Work Section Learning Objectives By the end of this section, you will be able to do the following: • Describe how pressure, volume, and temperature relate to one another and to work, based on the ideal gas law • Describe pressure–volume work • Describe the first law of thermodynamics verbally and mathematically • Solve problems involving the first law of thermodynamics Section Key Terms Boltzmann constant first law of thermodynamics ideal gas law internal energy pressure Pressure, Volume, Temperature, and the Ideal Gas Law Before covering the first law of thermodynamics, it is first important to understand the relationship between pressure, volume, and temperature. Pressure, P, is defined as where F is a force applied to an area, A, that is perpendicular to the force. Depending on the area over which it is exerted, a given force can have a significantly different effect, as shown in Figure 12.3. Figure 12.3 (a) Although the person being poked with the finger might be irritated, the force has little lasting effect. (b) In contrast, the same force applied to an area the size of the sharp end of a needle is great e

> **[Figure 209, Page 373]** A wide diagram or timeline (731×210px) related to: absolute, temperature, constant, called, Boltzmann, value. This image appears on page 373 of the textbook.
![Figure 209 - A wide diagram or timeline (731×210px) related to: absolute,](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0209_c8551e7f.png)
Figure 12.5 An expansion of a gas requires energy transfer to keep the pressure constant. Because pressure is constant, the work done is . Recall that the formula for work is We can rearrange the definition of pressure, to get an expression for force in terms of pressure. Substituting this expression for force into the definition of work, we get Because area multiplied by displacement is the change in volume, , the mathematical expression for pressure–volume work is Just as we say that work is force acting over a distance, for fluids, we can say that work is the pressure acting through the change in volume. For pressure–volume work, pressure is analogous to force, and volume is analogous to distance in the traditional definition of work.
Work from Expansion This video describes work from expansion (or pressure–volume work). Sal combines the equations and to get . Click to view content (https://www.openstax.org/l/28expansionWork)

Longitudinal waves are sometimes called compression waves or compressional waves, and transverse waves are sometimes called shear waves. Waves may be transverse, longitudinal, or a combination of the two. The waves on the strings of musical instruments are transverse (as shown in Figure 13.5), and so are electromagnetic waves, such as visible light. Sound waves in air and water are longitudinal. Their disturbances are periodic variations in pressure that are transmitted in fluids. 13.1 • Types of Waves
> **[Figure 224, Page 405]** A wide diagram or timeline (485×172px) related to: Longitudinal, Waves, Transverse, Mechanical, waves, categorized. This image appears on page 405 of the textbook.
![Figure 224 - A wide diagram or timeline (485×172px) related to: Longitudi](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0224_c8551e7f.png)
> **[Figure 225, Page 405]** A wide diagram or timeline (484×160px) related to: Longitudinal, Waves, Transverse, Mechanical, waves, categorized. This image appears on page 405 of the textbook.
![Figure 225 - A wide diagram or timeline (484×160px) related to: Longitudi](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0225_c8551e7f.png)

![Figure 175 - A figure or diagram (300×376px) related to: minutes, arrive,](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0175_c8551e7f.png)
Watt’s Steam Engine James Watt did not invent the steam engine, but by the time he was finished tinkering with it, it was more useful. The first steam engines were not only inefficient, they only produced a back and forth, or reciprocal, motion. This was natural because pistons move in and out as the pressure in the chamber changes. This limitation was okay for simple tasks like pumping water or mashing potatoes, but did not work so well for moving a train. Watt was able build a steam engine that converted reciprocal motion to circular motion. With that one innovation, the industrial revolution was off and running. The world would never be the same. One of Watt's steam engines is shown in Figure 9.5. The video that follows the figure explains the importance of the steam engine in the industrial revolution. Figure 9.5 A late version of the Watt steam engine. (Nehemiah Hawkins, Wikimedia Commons)
Watt's Role in the Industrial Revolution This video demonstrates how the watts that resulted from Watt's inventions helped make the industrial revolution possible and allowed England to enter a new historical era. Click to view content (https://www.youtube.com/embed/zhL5DCizj5c)
Which form of mechanical energy does the steam engine generate? a. Potential energy b. Kinetic energy c. Nuclear energy d. Solar energy Before proceeding, be sure you understand the distinctions among force, work, energy, and power. Force exerted on an object over a distance d

deep_worked_example.stem: 根据教材片段，设计一道关于「流体压强与流速关系」的现象解释或实验探究题。
deep_worked_example.solution_outline: 先描述现象或实验条件，再指出关键概念和证据，最后给出解释或结论。
deep_worked_example.evidence_excerpt: a. Energy can neither be created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction. b. If two systems, A and B, are in thermal equilibrium with each another, and B is in thermal equilibrium with a third system, C, then A is also in thermal equilibrium with C. c. Entropy of

errors.description: 只套用「流体压强与流速关系」相关公式，忽略物理情境、单位和适用条件。
实验分析时没有控制变量，导致无法判断因果关系。

exercises.stem: 围绕「流体压强与流速关系」，写出一个可观察现象，并说明其中涉及的物理量或规律。
exercises.answer: 应扣住课标要点：了解流体压强与流速的关系及其在生产生活中的应用。，说明现象、条件和规律。

exercises.stem: 如果要验证「流体压强与流速关系」相关规律，你会控制哪些变量？记录哪些数据？
exercises.answer: 需说明实验目的、控制变量、测量数据、可能误差和结论依据。

real_world: 观察家用电器、交通工具、运动器材或自然现象中与「流体压强与流速关系」相关的物理规律。
